events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: flange bolt-hole rotation wrong (two-holing missed). This is a non-compliant fit-up defect. Work is rough-complete, 70% complete until defects corrected. Need to assess.

Decision: fail because non-compliant (two-holing missed violates standard workmanship). Risk: medium? Could be low/medium. Flange bolt-hole rotation wrong can cause gasket seating issues, but not necessarily critical. I'll say medium.

Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.

Percent complete: 70.

Value: 0 (not asked).

Confidence: 0.9.

Findings: flange bolt-hole rotation incorrect, two-holing pattern missed.

Actions: break flange joint, rotate spool/flange to correct bolt-hole orientation, re-fit and re-align.

Rationale: ASME B31.3 and iso drawings require proper flange alignment and two-holing; incorrect rotation is non-compliant and must be corrected before closeout.

References: ASME B31.3, project iso drawings.
